questions.q: In the Ramayana, what is Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Ayodhya is the ancient royal capital city, the seat of Rama's dynasty.

questions.q: Of which kingdom is Ayodhya the capital?
questions.explanation: Ayodhya is the capital of the kingdom of Kosala.

questions.q: Which prince of the Ramayana was born in Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Rama, the hero of the Ramayana, was born in Ayodhya as a prince of Kosala.

questions.q: Who was the king of Ayodhya and the father of Rama?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha was the king of Ayodhya and the father of Rama.

questions.q: Along which river does Ayodhya stand?
questions.explanation: Ayodhya stands on the banks of the river Sarayu.

questions.q: For how many years did Rama leave Ayodhya during his exile?
questions.explanation: Rama left Ayodhya to spend fourteen years in exile in the forest.

questions.q: When Rama finally returned to Ayodhya, how was he received?
questions.explanation: Rama returned to Ayodhya in triumph and was joyfully welcomed by its people.

questions.q: Which of these brothers of Rama also belonged to the royal house of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Bharata was Rama's brother and a prince of the royal house of Ayodhya.

questions.q: What ceremony was being prepared for Rama in Ayodhya before his exile?
questions.explanation: Ayodhya was preparing Rama's installation as crown prince when the plan was overturned.

questions.q: Who ruled Ayodhya on Rama's behalf while Rama was in exile?
questions.explanation: Bharata governed Ayodhya in Rama's name while Rama was away in exile.

questions.q: To which famous dynasty did the kings of Ayodhya belong?
questions.explanation: The kings of Ayodhya belonged to the Ikshvaku, or Solar, dynasty.

questions.q: From where did Bharata rule the affairs of Ayodhya during Rama's exile?
options: From the nearby village of Nandigrama
questions.explanation: Bharata governed from Nandigrama, near Ayodhya, refusing to sit on the throne himself.

questions.q: What did Bharata place upon the throne of Ayodhya to represent Rama's authority?
questions.explanation: Bharata enshrined Rama's sandals on the throne of Ayodhya as a token of Rama's kingship.

questions.q: Who was the chief minister and charioteer of the king of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Sumantra was the trusted minister and charioteer of King Dasharatha of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Who was the royal preceptor (family guru) of the kings of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Sage Vasishtha was the family preceptor of the royal house of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Which queen of Ayodhya demanded that Rama be exiled?
questions.explanation: Queen Kaikeyi demanded Rama's exile, using the boons King Dasharatha had promised her.

questions.q: What happened to King Dasharatha in Ayodhya after Rama departed for the forest?
options: He abdicated and became a hermit
questions.explanation: Overcome with grief at Rama's exile, Dasharatha died in Ayodhya soon after.

questions.q: Which serving-woman of Ayodhya is blamed for poisoning Kaikeyi's mind against Rama?
questions.explanation: Manthara, Kaikeyi's hunchbacked maidservant, incited her against Rama in Ayodhya.

questions.q: Who was Rama's mother and the senior queen of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Kausalya, the eldest queen of Dasharatha, was Rama's mother.

questions.q: The triumphant return of Rama to Ayodhya is traditionally remembered by which festival?
questions.explanation: Rama's return to Ayodhya, greeted by rows of lamps, is celebrated as the festival of Diwali.

questions.q: From which city did Rama bring his bride Sita home to Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Rama married Sita in Mithila, the capital of Janaka, and brought her to Ayodhya.

questions.q: Who was sent by Rama as a messenger to assure Ayodhya of his coming return?
questions.explanation: Hanuman was sent ahead to Ayodhya to announce Rama's approaching return.

questions.q: Who is traditionally regarded as the founder of Ayodhya in the Ikshvaku line?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Manu, ancestor of the Ikshvaku kings, founded the city of Ayodhya.

questions.q: After Rama's reign, among whom did he traditionally divide and distribute the wider Kosala realm?
questions.explanation: Rama is said to have established his sons Lava and Kusha as rulers, dividing the Kosala lands between them.

questions.q: Which two boons granted by Dasharatha did Kaikeyi invoke in Ayodhya?
options: Rama's marriage and a great sacrifice
A treasury share and a new palace
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi used her two boons to demand Rama's fourteen-year exile and Bharata's installation as king.

questions.q: What name of Rama's great-grandfather is the source of the title 'Raghu' dynasty borne by Ayodhya's kings?
questions.explanation: The kings of Ayodhya are called the Raghu (Raghava) line after King Raghu, an illustrious ancestor of Rama.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, how does the opening canto describe Ayodhya's defenses and layout?
options: A ruined city of broken walls
A well-planned, prosperous city with moats and gates
A floating city upon the sea
questions.explanation: Valmiki's Bala Kanda praises Ayodhya as a prosperous, well-fortified city with broad roads, moats, and strong gates.

questions.q: According to tradition, what finally became of Ayodhya's people when Rama departed the world at the Sarayu?
options: They abandoned the city for Lanka
Many followed him into the river and to heaven
questions.explanation: Tradition relates that the people of Ayodhya followed Rama into the Sarayu and attained the celestial world with him.

questions.q: Whom did Rama appoint to rule in his name while he undertook the duties of kingship in Ayodhya, sending them to neighboring lands?
options: His sons, as rulers of new cities
His brothers Bharata and Shatrughna over other regions
questions.explanation: Rama entrusted regions such as the Sindhu-Sauvira and Madhura lands to his brothers Bharata and Shatrughna while he reigned in Ayodhya.

questions.q: What is the literal meaning of the name 'Ayodhya'?
options: The unconquerable / not to be warred against
questions.explanation: The name Ayodhya means 'unconquerable' or 'that which cannot be warred against', from a-yodhya.
